/**
 * hc_event_dispatcher.h
 *
 * HoloCanvas – Shared Event Dispatcher Module
 * --------------------------------------------
 * A generic, reusable event dispatcher that centralizes the Kafka event
 * consumption loop and dispatching logic. Services can register event handlers
 * for specific event types and the dispatcher will route incoming messages
 * to the appropriate handlers.
 *
 * This module aims to eliminate duplicated boilerplate code across services
 * like governance_hall, mint_factory, and muse_observer.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   1. Create a dispatcher with event_dispatcher_create()
 *   2. Register handlers for event types with event_dispatcher_register_handler()
 *   3. Start the event loop with event_dispatcher_run()
 *   4. Clean up with event_dispatcher_destroy()
 *
 * Copyright (c) 2024 The HoloCanvas Contributors
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 */

#ifndef HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H
#define HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stddef.h>
#include <stdbool.h>

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

/* Include common error types from the shared library */
#include "../common/errors.h"

/* Forward declaration for protobuf-c message base type */
#ifndef PROTOBUF_C__MESSAGE__DEFINED
typedef struct ProtobufCMessage ProtobufCMessage;
#endif

/* ============================================================================
 * Type Definitions
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Function pointer type for event handlers.
 *
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "VOTE_CAST")
 * @param payload      Pointer to raw JSON payload string
 * @param payload_len  Length of the payload
 * @param user_context User-defined context pointer passed during registration
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate hc_error_t on failure
 */
typedef hc_error_t (*event_handler_func_t)(const char *event_type,
                                            const char *payload,
                                            size_t payload_len,
                                            void *user_context);

/**
 * Opaque struct representing the event dispatcher context.
 * The internal implementation is hidden from consumers.
 */
typedef struct EventDispatcher EventDispatcher;

/**
 * Configuration structure for creating an event dispatcher.
 */
typedef struct {
    const char *kafka_brokers;    /**< Comma-separated list of broker addresses */
    const char *topic;            /**< Kafka topic to subscribe to */
    const char *group_id;         /**< Consumer group identifier */
    void *user_context;           /**< User context passed to all handlers */
    int poll_timeout_ms;          /**< Kafka poll timeout in milliseconds (default: 500) */
    bool enable_auto_commit;      /**< Enable auto-commit of offsets (default: true) */
} EventDispatcherConfig;

/* ============================================================================
 * Public API
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Create a new event dispatcher instance.
 *
 * The dispatcher will connect to the specified Kafka brokers and subscribe
 * to the given topic. The user_context will be passed to all registered
 * event handlers.
 *
 * @param config Configuration structure with connection parameters
 *
 * @return Pointer to the newly created dispatcher, or NULL on failure
 */
EventDispatcher *event_dispatcher_create(const EventDispatcherConfig *config);

/**
 * Register an event handler for a specific event type.
 *
 * Multiple handlers can be registered for the same event type - they will
 * all be invoked when an event of that type is received.
 *
 * @param dispatcher   Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "PROPOSAL_CREATED")
 * @param handler      Function pointer to the event handler
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate error code on failure
 */
hc_error_t event_dispatcher_register_handler(EventDispatcher *dispatcher,
                                              const char *event_type,
                                              event_handler_func_t handler);

/**
 * Start the event dispatcher's main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function will block and continuously poll Kafka for messages,
 * deserializing them and dispatching to the appropriate registered handlers.
 * The loop will continue until event_dispatcher_stop() is called or a
 * fatal error occurs.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_run(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Signal the dispatcher to stop its main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function is thread-safe and can be called from a signal handler
 * or another thread. The event_dispatcher_run() function will return
 * after completing any in-progress message processing.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_stop(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Destroy the event dispatcher and release all resources.
 *
 * This function will stop the dispatcher if it's still running,
 * close the Kafka connection, and free all allocated memory including
 * the handler registry.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance (can be NULL)
 */
void event_dispatcher_destroy(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Get the user context associated with the dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return The user context pointer, or NULL if dispatcher is NULL
 */
void *event_dispatcher_get_context(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Check if the dispatcher is currently running.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return true if running, false otherwise
 */
bool event_dispatcher_is_running(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Install default signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that will stop
 * the given dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance to stop on signal
 */
void event_dispatcher_install_signal_handlers(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

#ifdef __cplusplus
} /* extern "C" */
#endif

#endif /* HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H */

/**
 * hc_event_dispatcher.h
 *
 * HoloCanvas – Shared Event Dispatcher Module
 * --------------------------------------------
 * A generic, reusable event dispatcher that centralizes the Kafka event
 * consumption loop and dispatching logic. Services can register event handlers
 * for specific event types and the dispatcher will route incoming messages
 * to the appropriate handlers.
 *
 * This module aims to eliminate duplicated boilerplate code across services
 * like governance_hall, mint_factory, and muse_observer.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   1. Create a dispatcher with event_dispatcher_create()
 *   2. Register handlers for event types with event_dispatcher_register_handler()
 *   3. Start the event loop with event_dispatcher_run()
 *   4. Clean up with event_dispatcher_destroy()
 *
 * Copyright (c) 2024 The HoloCanvas Contributors
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 */

#ifndef HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H
#define HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stddef.h>
#include <stdbool.h>

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

/* Include common error types from the shared library */
#include "../common/errors.h"

/* Forward declaration for protobuf-c message base type */
#ifndef PROTOBUF_C__MESSAGE__DEFINED
typedef struct ProtobufCMessage ProtobufCMessage;
#endif

/* ============================================================================
 * Type Definitions
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Function pointer type for event handlers.
 *
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "VOTE_CAST")
 * @param payload      Pointer to raw JSON payload string
 * @param payload_len  Length of the payload
 * @param user_context User-defined context pointer passed during registration
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate hc_error_t on failure
 */
typedef hc_error_t (*event_handler_func_t)(const char *event_type,
                                            const char *payload,
                                            size_t payload_len,
                                            void *user_context);

/**
 * Opaque struct representing the event dispatcher context.
 * The internal implementation is hidden from consumers.
 */
typedef struct EventDispatcher EventDispatcher;

/**
 * Configuration structure for creating an event dispatcher.
 */
typedef struct {
    const char *kafka_brokers;    /**< Comma-separated list of broker addresses */
    const char *topic;            /**< Kafka topic to subscribe to */
    const char *group_id;         /**< Consumer group identifier */
    void *user_context;           /**< User context passed to all handlers */
    int poll_timeout_ms;          /**< Kafka poll timeout in milliseconds (default: 500) */
    bool enable_auto_commit;      /**< Enable auto-commit of offsets (default: true) */
} EventDispatcherConfig;

/* ============================================================================
 * Public API
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Create a new event dispatcher instance.
 *
 * The dispatcher will connect to the specified Kafka brokers and subscribe
 * to the given topic. The user_context will be passed to all registered
 * event handlers.
 *
 * @param config Configuration structure with connection parameters
 *
 * @return Pointer to the newly created dispatcher, or NULL on failure
 */
EventDispatcher *event_dispatcher_create(const EventDispatcherConfig *config);

/**
 * Register an event handler for a specific event type.
 *
 * Multiple handlers can be registered for the same event type - they will
 * all be invoked when an event of that type is received.
 *
 * @param dispatcher   Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "PROPOSAL_CREATED")
 * @param handler      Function pointer to the event handler
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate error code on failure
 */
hc_error_t event_dispatcher_register_handler(EventDispatcher *dispatcher,
                                              const char *event_type,
                                              event_handler_func_t handler);

/**
 * Start the event dispatcher's main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function will block and continuously poll Kafka for messages,
 * deserializing them and dispatching to the appropriate registered handlers.
 * The loop will continue until event_dispatcher_stop() is called or a
 * fatal error occurs.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_run(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Signal the dispatcher to stop its main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function is thread-safe and can be called from a signal handler
 * or another thread. The event_dispatcher_run() function will return
 * after completing any in-progress message processing.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_stop(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Destroy the event dispatcher and release all resources.
 *
 * This function will stop the dispatcher if it's still running,
 * close the Kafka connection, and free all allocated memory including
 * the handler registry.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance (can be NULL)
 */
void event_dispatcher_destroy(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Get the user context associated with the dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return The user context pointer, or NULL if dispatcher is NULL
 */
void *event_dispatcher_get_context(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Check if the dispatcher is currently running.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return true if running, false otherwise
 */
bool event_dispatcher_is_running(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Install default signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that will stop
 * the given dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance to stop on signal
 */
void event_dispatcher_install_signal_handlers(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

#ifdef __cplusplus
} /* extern "C" */
#endif

#endif /* HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H */

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HoloCanvas :: shared :: CMake build configuration
#
# This CMakeLists.txt drives the compilation of the reusable "shared" component that is linked into all HoloCanvas
# micro-services.  It bundles common primitives such as:
#   • cryptographic helpers (OpenSSL wrappers)
#   • binary-safe serialization utilities (Protocol-Buffers / gRPC)
#   • event-stream abstractions (Kafka)
#   • event dispatcher (NEW: centralized event handling)
#   • state-machine helpers
#   • common error/ logging facilities
#
# The file is intentionally opinionated and enables modern-C best practices out of the box: C11, LTO, enable-all-warnings,
# sanitizers, unit-tests, documentation, packaging, and export-targets for downstream consumers.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20 FATAL_ERROR)

# 1. Project basics ---------------------------------------------------------------------
project(
    HoloCanvasShared
    VERSION 0.4.0
    DESCRIPTION "Common runtime & utils for the HoloCanvas micro-gallery blockchain (NFT)"
    LANGUAGES C
)

# 2. Build options ----------------------------------------------------------------------
option(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS "Build shared libraries instead of static ones" ON)
option(ENABLE_SANITIZER  "Enable UBSan / ASan checks (Debug only)"       OFF)
option(ENABLE_LTO        "Enable Link-Time-Optimization (Release only)" ON)
option(ENABLE_TESTS      "Build unit tests with CTest / Catch2"          ON)
option(ENABLE_COVERAGE   "Enable gcov/llvm-cov instrumentation"          OFF)

# 3. Toolchain sanity checks ------------------------------------------------------------
if(CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR STREQUAL CMAKE_BINARY_DIR)
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "In-source builds are disabled. Please create a separate build directory:\n"
        "  mkdir build && cd build && cmake .."
    )
endif()

# 4. Compiler setup ---------------------------------------------------------------------
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS OFF)

function(holo_enable_all_warnings TARGET)
    if (MSVC)
        target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE /W4 /WX)
    else()
        target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE
            -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror
            -Wshadow -Wpointer-arith -Wcast-align
            -Wstrict-prototypes -Wmissing-prototypes
            -Wwrite-strings -Wconversion
        )
    endif()
endfunction()

# 5. External dependencies --------------------------------------------------------------
find_package(Threads       REQUIRED)
find_package(OpenSSL       REQUIRED)
find_package(Protobuf      REQUIRED)
find_package(gRPC          REQUIRED)
find_package(ZLIB          REQUIRED)
# Jansson for JSON parsing (required by event_dispatcher)
find_package(PkgConfig REQUIRED)
pkg_check_modules(JANSSON REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET jansson)
# Kafka client (librdkafka) is shipped as a submodule
add_subdirectory(third_party/librdkafka EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)

# 6. Source layout ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Include the new event_dispatcher module
set(EVENT_DISPATCHER_SRCS
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/hc_event_dispatcher.c"
)

set(EVENT_DISPATCHER_HDRS
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/hc_event_dispatcher.h"
)

file(GLOB_RECURSE HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC  CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/crypto_wrapper/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/kafka_client/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/protocol/*.c"
)

file(GLOB_RECURSE HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/crypto_wrapper/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/kafka_client/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/protocol/*.h"
)

# Combine all sources
list(APPEND HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC ${EVENT_DISPATCHER_SRCS})
list(APPEND HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS ${EVENT_DISPATCHER_HDRS})

# 7. Auto-generate a version header -----------------------------------------------------
set(GIT_DESCRIBE "unknown")
find_package(Git QUIET)
if(GIT_FOUND)
    execute_process(
        COMMAND "${GIT_EXECUTABLE}" -C "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}" describe --tags --always --dirty
        OUTPUT_VARIABLE GIT_DESCRIBE
        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
    )
endif()

configure_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/version.h.in"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated/hc_version.h"
    @ONLY
)

# 8. Library target ---------------------------------------------------------------------
add_library(hc_shared ${HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC} ${HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS})
add_library(HoloCanvas::Shared ALIAS hc_shared)

target_include_directories(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}>
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated>
        $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>
)

target_link_libraries(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        OpenSSL::SSL
        OpenSSL::Crypto
        Threads::Threads
        ZLIB::ZLIB
        Protobuf::protobuf
        gRPC::grpc
        PkgConfig::JANSSON
    PRIVATE
        rdkafka
)

holo_enable_all_warnings(hc_shared)

target_compile_definitions(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        $<$<CONFIG:Debug>:HC_DEBUG=1>
        HC_VERSION=\"${PROJECT_VERSION}\"
        HC_GIT_DESCRIBE=\"${GIT_DESCRIBE}\"
)

# 8.1 Sanitizers ------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(ENABLE_SANITIZER AND CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang|GNU")
    target_compile_options(hc_shared PUBLIC -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
    target_link_options  (hc_shared PUBLIC -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
endif()

# 8.2 LTO ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
include(CheckIPOSupported)
if(ENABLE_LTO)
    check_ipo_supported(RESULT LTO_OK)
    if(LTO_OK)
        message(STATUS "Enabling LTO")
        set_property(TARGET hc_shared PROPERTY INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION_RELEASE TRUE)
    endif()
endif()

# 8.3 Coverage --------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(ENABLE_COVERAGE AND CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang|GNU")
    target_compile_options(hc_shared PUBLIC -O0 -g --coverage)
    target_link_options  (hc_shared PUBLIC --coverage)
endif()

# 9. Installation -----------------------------------------------------------------------
include(GNUInstallDirs)
install(
    TARGETS hc_shared
    EXPORT  HoloCanvasSharedTargets
    ARCHIVE  DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    LIBRARY  DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME  DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)

install(
    DIRECTORY include/
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
    FILES_MATCHING PATTERN "*.h"
)

# Install event_dispatcher headers
install(
    DIRECTORY event_dispatcher/
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}/shared/event_dispatcher
    FILES_MATCHING PATTERN "*.h"
)

# Install common headers
install(
    DIRECTORY common/
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}/shared/common
    FILES_MATCHING PATTERN "*.h"
)

install(
    FILES "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated/hc_version.h"
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}/holocanvas
)

install(
    EXPORT HoloCanvasSharedTargets
    NAMESPACE HoloCanvas::
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/HoloCanvas
)

include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers)
write_basic_package_version_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/HoloCanvasSharedConfigVersion.cmake"
    VERSION ${PROJECT_VERSION}
    COMPATIBILITY SameMajorVersion
)

configure_package_config_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/HoloCanvasSharedConfig.cmake.in"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/HoloCanvasSharedConfig.cmake"
    INSTALL_DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/HoloCanvas
)

install(
    FILES
        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/HoloCanvasSharedConfig.cmake"
        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/HoloCanvasSharedConfigVersion.cmake"
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/HoloCanvas
)

# 10. Testing ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(ENABLE_TESTS)
    enable_testing()
    add_subdirectory(test)
endif()

# 11. Developer helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------
# clang-format target -------------------------------------------------------------------
find_program(CLANG_FORMAT_EXE NAMES clang-format)
if(CLANG_FORMAT_EXE)
    file(GLOB_RECURSE C_SOURCES_CLANGFORMAT
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/*.c"
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/*.h"
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/*.c"
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/*.h"
    )
    add_custom_target(
        clang-format
        COMMAND ${CLANG_FORMAT_EXE} -i ${C_SOURCES_CLANGFORMAT}
        COMMENT "Running clang-format on source tree"
    )
endif()

# cppcheck / static-analysis -------------------------------------------------------------
find_program(CPPCHECK_EXE NAMES cppcheck)
if(CPPCHECK_EXE)
    add_custom_target(
        static-analysis
        COMMAND ${CPPCHECK_EXE} --enable=all --inconclusive --force
                --language=c --std=c11
                -I ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include
                -I ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}
                ${HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC}
        COMMENT "Running CppCheck analysis"
    )
endif()

# 12. Summary ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
message(STATUS "----------------------------------------------------------------------")
message(STATUS "HoloCanvas::Shared Configuration:")
message(STATUS "  Build type           : ${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE}")
message(STATUS "  Install prefix       : ${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}")
message(STATUS "  Git describe         : ${GIT_DESCRIBE}")
message(STATUS "  Sanitizer enabled    : ${ENABLE_SANITIZER}")
message(STATUS "  LTO enabled          : ${ENABLE_LTO}")
message(STATUS "  Coverage enabled     : ${ENABLE_COVERAGE}")
message(STATUS "  Tests enabled        : ${ENABLE_TESTS}")
message(STATUS "  Event Dispatcher     : ENABLED (NEW)")
message(STATUS "----------------------------------------------------------------------")

## Task Title

Refactor Disparate Event Handling Logic into a Shared Event Dispatcher Module

## Description

The HoloCanvas system is composed of several microservices that communicate via a Kafka message bus. Currently, multiple services (governance_hall, mint_factory, muse_observer) contain duplicated, boilerplate code for consuming, deserializing, and dispatching these events. This approach is inefficient, error-prone, and makes adding new event types difficult. The goal is to refactor this functionality into a single, generic, and reusable 'Event Dispatcher' module within the `shared` library. This will centralize the event consumption loop and allow each service to simply register its specific event handlers.

## Your Task

Your task is to implement a major architectural refactoring by creating a shared event dispatcher. You must centralize the event handling logic currently duplicated across multiple services.

**Detailed Requirements:**

1.  **Create a New Shared Module:**
    *   In the `HoloCanvas/shared/` directory, create a new subdirectory named `event_dispatcher`.
    *   Inside this new directory, create two new files: `hc_event_dispatcher.h` and `hc_event_dispatcher.c`.

2.  **Define the Dispatcher Interface (`hc_event_dispatcher.h`):**
    *   Define a function pointer type for event handlers: `typedef hc_error_t (*event_handler_func_t)(const ProtobufCMessage* msg, void* user_context);`.
    *   Define an opaque struct for the event dispatcher context: `typedef struct EventDispatcher EventDispatcher;`.
    *   Declare the public API for the dispatcher:
        *   `EventDispatcher* event_dispatcher_create(const char* kafka_brokers, const char* topic, const char* group_id, void* user_context);`
        *   `hc_error_t event_dispatcher_register_handler(EventDispatcher* dispatcher, const char* event_type_key, event_handler_func_t handler);`
        *   `void event_dispatcher_run(EventDispatcher* dispatcher);` (This will contain the main consumption loop).
        *   `void event_dispatcher_destroy(EventDispatcher* dispatcher);`

3.  **Implement the Dispatcher Logic (`hc_event_dispatcher.c`):**
    *   Implement the functions declared in the header.
    *   The implementation should use a hash map or a similar data structure to store the mapping from `event_type_key` strings to `event_handler_func_t` function pointers.
    *   The `event_dispatcher_run` function must encapsulate the logic for connecting to Kafka (using the existing `hc_kafka` client), consuming messages in a loop, deserializing the message (assuming a generic wrapper proto with an `event_type` field), looking up the handler in the registry, and invoking it with the message payload and user context.

4.  **Refactor Consumer Services:**
    *   Modify the following services to use the new event dispatcher: `governance_hall`, `mint_factory`, and `muse_observer`.
    *   **In each service's `main.c`:**
        *   Remove the existing Kafka connection and consumption loop.
        *   Instantiate the new `EventDispatcher` using `event_dispatcher_create()`.
        *   Register all service-specific event handlers using `event_dispatcher_register_handler()`.
        *   Start the event processing by calling `event_dispatcher_run()`.
        *   Ensure `event_dispatcher_destroy()` is called on shutdown.
    *   **In each service's event handling file (`governance_hall/src/event_handler.c`, `mint_factory/src/event_handler.c`, `muse_observer/src/event_listener.c`):**
        *   Remove the main dispatching function (e.g., the large `switch` statement that routes events).
        *   Ensure the individual handler functions (e.g., `handle_proposal_created`, `handle_artifact_minted`) are modified to match the `event_handler_func_t` signature and are made visible to `main.c` (i.e., not `static` if they were before, and declared in a local header if necessary).

5.  **Update Build System:**
    *   Modify `HoloCanvas/shared/CMakeLists.txt` to include the new `event_dispatcher` source files in the `shared_lib` target.
    *   Verify that the `CMakeLists.txt` files for `governance_hall`, `mint_factory`, and `muse_observer` correctly link against the `shared_lib`.

## Expected Approach

An expert developer would approach this systematically:

1.  **Analysis & Interface Design:** First, inspect the event handling logic in `governance_hall/src/event_handler.c`, `mint_factory/src/event_handler.c`, and `muse_observer/src/event_listener.c` to identify the common pattern: a Kafka consumer loop, message deserialization, a large switch/if-else block on an event type field, and delegation to a specific function. Based on this, they would design the clean, generic interface in `hc_event_dispatcher.h`.

2.  **Implement the Core Abstraction:** Implement the `hc_event_dispatcher.c` module. This is the core of the task. They would likely use a robust hash map implementation for the handler registry. The `event_dispatcher_run` function would be carefully crafted to handle the consumer loop, error conditions, and graceful shutdown.

     2→/**
     3→ * errors.h
     4→ * HoloCanvas – Common error definitions and helpers
     5→ *
     6→ * This header is consumed by every micro-service that makes up the HoloCanvas
     7→ * blockchain NFT platform.  It provides:
     8→ *
     9→ *   • A strictly-typed, 32-bit error code layout that unifies error handling
    10→ *     across service boundaries.
    11→ *   • Sub-system identifiers (Cryptograph, LedgerCore, etc.) so that any error
    12→ *     can be traced back to its originating bounded context.
    13→ *   • Severity levels that allow transport-agnostic log routing (stderr,
    14→ *     syslog, Kafka topic, OpenTelemetry, …).
    15→ *   • Convenience macros for building, de-constructing and propagating errors
    16→ *     with zero boilerplate.
    17→ *
    18→ * Copyright (c) 2023-2024 The HoloCanvas contributors
    19→ * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
    20→ */
    21→
    22→#ifndef HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_COMMON_ERRORS_H
    23→#define HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_COMMON_ERRORS_H
    24→
    25→/*───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────*/
    26→#include <stdint.h>
    27→#include <stdio.h>
    28→
    29→#ifdef __cplusplus
    30→extern "C" {
    31→#endif
    32→/*───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────*/
    33→
    34→/**
    35→ * 32-bit error code layout
    36→ *
    37→ *   31 ── 24 │ Sub-system  (8 bits)
    38→ *   23 ── 20 │ Severity    (4 bits)
    39→ *   19 ── 16 │ Reserved    (4 bits)
    40→ *   15 ──  0 │ Detail      (16 bits)
    41→ */
    42→typedef uint32_t hc_error_t;
    43→
    44→/*===========================================================================*/
    45→/* Sub-system identifiers (8 bits)                                           */
    46→/*===========================================================================*/
    47→typedef enum {
    48→    HC_SUBSYS_UNDEFINED     = 0x00,
    49→    HC_SUBSYS_CRYPTOGRAPH   = 0x01,
    50→    HC_SUBSYS_LEDGER_CORE   = 0x02,
    51→    HC_SUBSYS_MINT_FACTORY  = 0x03,
    52→    HC_SUBSYS_GALLERY_GATE  = 0x04,
    53→    HC_SUBSYS_DEFI_GARDEN   = 0x05,
    54→    HC_SUBSYS_ORACLE_BRIDGE = 0x06,
    55→    HC_SUBSYS_WALLET_PROXY  = 0x07,
    56→    HC_SUBSYS_GOVERNANCE    = 0x08,
    57→    HC_SUBSYS_COMMON        = 0xFE,   /* Utility & shared libs  */
    58→    HC_SUBSYS_UNKNOWN       = 0xFF
    59→} hc_subsys_t;
    60→
    61→/*===========================================================================*/
    62→/* Severity levels (4 bits)                                                  */
    63→/*===========================================================================*/
    64→typedef enum {
    65→    HC_SEV_SUCCESS  = 0x0,   /* Non-error, success                      */
    66→    HC_SEV_INFO     = 0x1,   /* Informative message, no action required */
    67→    HC_SEV_WARNING  = 0x2,   /* Recoverable error                       */
    68→    HC_SEV_ERROR    = 0x3,   /* Non-fatal error                         */
    69→    HC_SEV_FATAL    = 0x4    /* Unrecoverable, must abort               */
    70→} hc_sev_t;
    71→
    72→/*===========================================================================*/
    73→/* Macro helpers                                                             */
    74→/*===========================================================================*/
    75→#define HC_ERROR_PACK(subsys, sev, detail) \
    76→    ((((uint32_t)(subsys)  & 0xFFu) << 24) | \
    77→     (((uint32_t)(sev)     & 0x0Fu) << 20) | \
    78→     (((uint32_t)(detail)  & 0xFFFFu)))
    79→
    80→#define HC_ERROR_SUBSYS(code)   ((hc_subsys_t)(((code) >> 24) & 0xFFu))
    81→#define HC_ERROR_SEVERITY(code) ((hc_sev_t)   (((code) >> 20) & 0x0Fu))
    82→#define HC_ERROR_DETAIL(code)   ((uint16_t)   ((code) & 0xFFFFu))
    83→
    84→#define HC_OK  ((hc_error_t)0)  /* Convenience alias for success */
    85→
    86→/*===========================================================================*/
    87→/* Common detail codes (16 bits)                                             */
    88→/* Service-specific headers should extend their own detail ranges starting   */
    89→/* at 0x0100 to avoid overlap with these commons.                            */
    90→/*===========================================================================*/
    91→typedef enum {
    92→    HC_EC_SUCCESS             = 0x0000,
    93→    HC_EC_INVALID_ARGUMENT    = 0x0001,
    94→    HC_EC_OUT_OF_MEMORY       = 0x0002,
    95→    HC_EC_IO_ERROR            = 0x0003,
    96→    HC_EC_TIMEOUT             = 0x0004,
    97→    HC_EC_NOT_IMPLEMENTED     = 0x0005,
    98→    HC_EC_DATA_CORRUPT        = 0x0006,
    99→    HC_EC_PERMISSION_DENIED   = 0x0007,
   100→    HC_EC_STATE_VIOLATION     = 0x0008,
   101→    HC_EC_DEPENDENCY_FAILURE  = 0x0009,
   102→    HC_EC_NETWORK_UNREACHABLE = 0x000A,
   103→    HC_EC_ASSERTION_FAILED    = 0x000B,
   104→    HC_EC_UNKNOWN             = 0xFFFF
   105→} hc_ec_t;
   106→
   107→/*===========================================================================*/
   108→/* Public API                                                                */
   109→/*===========================================================================*/
   110→
   111→/**
   112→ * Convert an error code into a human-readable, short message.
   113→ *
   114→ * Implemented inline for header-only availability, but backed by an internal
   115→ * constant table to keep it lightweight.  The resulting pointer is to a
   116→ * read-only static string—do NOT free it.
   117→ */
   118→static inline const char *
   119→hc_error_str(hc_error_t code)
   120→{
   121→    switch (HC_ERROR_DETAIL(code)) {
   122→        case HC_EC_SUCCESS:             return "Success";
   123→        case HC_EC_INVALID_ARGUMENT:    return "Invalid argument";
   124→        case HC_EC_OUT_OF_MEMORY:       return "Out of memory";
   125→        case HC_EC_IO_ERROR:            return "I/O error";
   126→        case HC_EC_TIMEOUT:             return "Operation timed out";
   127→        case HC_EC_NOT_IMPLEMENTED:     return "Not implemented";
   128→        case HC_EC_DATA_CORRUPT:        return "Data corrupt";
   129→        case HC_EC_PERMISSION_DENIED:   return "Permission denied";
   130→        case HC_EC_STATE_VIOLATION:     return "Invalid state for operation";
   131→        case HC_EC_DEPENDENCY_FAILURE:  return "Dependency failure";
   132→        case HC_EC_NETWORK_UNREACHABLE: return "Network unreachable";
   133→        case HC_EC_ASSERTION_FAILED:    return "Assertion failed";
   134→        case HC_EC_UNKNOWN:             /* fallthrough */
   135→        default:                        return "Unknown error";
   136→    }
   137→}
   138→
   139→/**
   140→ * Print a formatted error to the provided FILE* stream.
   141→ *
   142→ * Example:
   143→ *   hc_error_t err = some_function();
   144→ *   if (err != HC_OK) hc_error_fprint(stderr, err, __FILE__, __LINE__, "while opening wallet");
   145→ */
   146→static inline void
   147→hc_error_fprint(FILE *stream, hc_error_t code,
   148→                const char *file, int line, const char *context)
   149→{
   150→    fprintf(stream,
   151→            "[%s] %s (subsys=%02X sev=%u detail=%04X) at %s:%d\n",
   152→            context ? context : "context-none",
   153→            hc_error_str(code),
   154→            HC_ERROR_SUBSYS(code),
   155→            HC_ERROR_SEVERITY(code),
   156→            HC_ERROR_DETAIL(code),
   157→            file, line);
   158→}
   159→
   160→/*===========================================================================*/
   161→/* Convenience macros for ergonomic error handling                           */
   162→/*===========================================================================*/
   163→
   164→/* Wrap a call that returns hc_error_t; if it is not HC_OK, propagate upward */
   165→#define HC_TRY(call)                           \
   166→    do {                                       \
   167→        hc_error_t _e = (call);                \
   168→        if (_e != HC_OK) {                     \
   169→            return _e;                         \
   170→        }                                      \
   171→    } while (0)
   172→
   173→/*
   174→ * Similar to HC_TRY, but logs the error with the current location before
   175→ * bubbling it up.
   176→ */
   177→#ifndef HC_ERRLOG_STREAM
   178→#  define HC_ERRLOG_STREAM stderr
   179→#endif
   180→
   181→#define HC_TRY_LOG(call, ctx)                                  \
   182→    do {                                                       \
   183→        hc_error_t _e = (call);                                \
   184→        if (_e != HC_OK) {                                     \
   185→            hc_error_fprint(HC_ERRLOG_STREAM, _e,              \
   186→                            __FILE__, __LINE__, (ctx));        \
   187→            return _e;                                         \
   188→        }                                                      \
   189→    } while (0)
   190→
   191→/*
   192→ * Assert-like helper that returns HC_ASSERTION_FAILED on failure rather than
   193→ * aborting the process, allowing higher layers to decide how to handle it.
   194→ */
   195→#define HC_ENSURE(expr)                                                    \
   196→    do {                                                                   \
   197→        if (!(expr)) {                                                     \
   198→            hc_error_t _e = HC_ERROR_PACK(HC_SUBSYS_COMMON,                \
   199→                                          HC_SEV_FATAL,                    \
   200→                                          HC_EC_ASSERTION_FAILED);         \
   201→            hc_error_fprint(HC_ERRLOG_STREAM, _e,                          \
   202→                            __FILE__, __LINE__, "HC_ENSURE");              \
   203→            return _e;                                                     \
   204→        }                                                                  \
   205→    } while (0)
   206→
   207→/*===========================================================================*/
   208→#ifdef __cplusplus
   209→} /* extern "C" */
   210→#endif
   211→#endif /* HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_COMMON_ERRORS_H */

1→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
     2→# HoloCanvas :: Governance-Hall Micro-Service
     3→#
     4→# CMake build recipe for the Governance-Hall component.  This micro-service coordinates DAO-style on-chain governance
     5→# around evolving NFT artifacts.  It links against the shared “canvas_core” lib for cryptographic utilities as well as
     6→# third-party libraries for gRPC, protobuf-c, Kafka, and OpenSSL.  Unit-tests are driven via CMocka, and packaging is
     7→# handled through CPack.
     8→#
     9→# Target layout
    10→#   /src              → C source files
    11→#   /include          → Public headers for other services
    12→#   /proto            → gRPC / protobuf contracts (compiled to C using protobuf-c + gRPC-c)
    13→#   /tests            → Unit/integration tests
    14→#
    15→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
    18→project(holo_governance_hall C CXX)   # Build in C11 but allow C++ for third-party headers
    19→
    20→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21→# Build Options
    22→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23→
    24→option(ENABLE_LTO           "Enable Link-Time-Optimization"            ON)
    25→option(ENABLE_CLANG_TIDY    "Run clang-tidy static analysis"           OFF)
    26→option(ENABLE_TESTING       "Build unit / integration tests"           ON)
    27→option(ENABLE_COVERAGE      "Enable coverage flags (GCC/Clang only)"   OFF)
    28→
    29→set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
    30→set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
    31→set(CMAKE_POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE ON)
    32→
    33→# Warn aggressively.
    34→if(NOT MSVC)
    35→    add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Werror -Wshadow -Wpedantic -Wmissing-prototypes
    36→                        -Wformat=2 -Wno-unused-parameter)
    37→else()
    38→    add_compile_options(/W4 /WX)
    39→endif()
    40→
    41→# Coverage flags.
    42→if(ENABLE_COVERAGE AND CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "GNU|Clang")
    43→    add_compile_options(--coverage -O0)
    44→    list(APPEND EXTRA_LINK_FLAGS --coverage)
    45→endif()
    46→
    47→# LTO flags.
    48→include(CheckIPOSupported)
    49→if(ENABLE_LTO)
    50→    check_ipo_supported(RESULT IPO_SUPPORTED OUTPUT IPO_ERROR)
    51→    if(IPO_SUPPORTED)
    52→        set(CMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION TRUE)
    53→    else()
    54→        message(WARNING "IPO/LTO not supported: ${IPO_ERROR}")
    55→    endif()
    56→endif()
    57→
    58→# clang-tidy
    59→if(ENABLE_CLANG_TIDY)
    60→    find_program(CLANG_TIDY_EXE NAMES clang-tidy)
    61→    if(CLANG_TIDY_EXE)
    62→        set(CMAKE_C_CLANG_TIDY ${CLANG_TIDY_EXE})
    63→    else()
    64→        message(WARNING "clang-tidy not found; disabling static analysis.")
    65→    endif()
    66→endif()
    67→
    68→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69→# Dependencies
    70→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    71→
    72→include(FetchContent)
    73→
    74→# ---- Protobuf-C ----
    75→find_package(ProtobufC REQUIRED)
    76→# ---- gRPC-C (assumes system install, e.g., via vcpkg or distro) ----
    77→find_library(GRPC_C grpc_c REQUIRED)
    78→# ---- Kafka (librdkafka) ----
    79→find_package(PkgConfig REQUIRED)
    80→pkg_check_modules(RDKAFKA REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET librdkafka)
    81→# ---- OpenSSL ----
    82→find_package(OpenSSL REQUIRED)
    83→
    84→# ---- Canvas Core (shared internal library) ----
    85→# Expect installed to the build tree’s parent or fetched via FetchContent.
    86→FetchContent_Declare(
    87→    canvas_core
    88→    GIT_REPOSITORY  https://github.com/holocanvas/canvas_core.git
    89→    GIT_TAG         v1.4.2
    90→)
    91→FetchContent_MakeAvailable(canvas_core)
    92→
    93→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    94→# Proto / gRPC code generation
    95→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→
    97→file(GLOB PROTO_FILES "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/proto/*.proto")
    98→
    99→# Generate C bindings using protobuf-c.
   100→set(PROTO_C_OUT "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated")
   101→file(MAKE_DIRECTORY "${PROTO_C_OUT}")
   102→
   103→foreach(proto ${PROTO_FILES})
   104→    get_filename_component(proto_name ${proto} NAME_WE)
   105→    add_custom_command(
   106→        OUTPUT  "${PROTO_C_OUT}/${proto_name}.pb-c.c"
   107→                "${PROTO_C_OUT}/${proto_name}.pb-c.h"
   108→        COMMAND protoc
   109→                --proto_path=${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/proto
   110→                --c_out=${PROTO_C_OUT}
   111→                --grpc_out=${PROTO_C_OUT}
   112→                --plugin=protoc-gen-grpc=$(which grpc_c_plugin)
   113→                ${proto}
   114→        DEPENDS ${proto}
   115→        COMMENT "Generating C sources from ${proto}"
   116→        VERBATIM
   117→    )
   118→    list(APPEND GENERATED_PROTO_SRCS "${PROTO_C_OUT}/${proto_name}.pb-c.c")
   119→    list(APPEND GENERATED_PROTO_HDRS "${PROTO_C_OUT}/${proto_name}.pb-c.h")
   120→endforeach()
   121→
   122→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   123→# Library Target: governance_hall
   124→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→
   126→file(GLOB_RECURSE GOV_HALL_SRCS
   127→     CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
   128→     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/*.c"
   129→     "${GENERATED_PROTO_SRCS}"
   130→)
   131→
   132→add_library(governance_hall STATIC
   133→    ${GOV_HALL_SRCS}
   134→    ${GENERATED_PROTO_HDRS}
   135→)
   136→
   137→target_include_directories(governance_hall
   138→    PUBLIC
   139→        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
   140→        $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>
   141→    PRIVATE
   142→        ${PROTO_C_OUT}
   143→)
   144→
   145→target_link_libraries(governance_hall
   146→    PRIVATE
   147→        canvas_core
   148→        ProtobufC::ProtobufC
   149→        ${GRPC_C}
   150→        PkgConfig::RDKAFKA
   151→        OpenSSL::SSL
   152→        OpenSSL::Crypto
   153→        ${EXTRA_LINK_FLAGS}
   154→)
   155→
   156→set_target_properties(governance_hall PROPERTIES
   157→    VERSION   1.2.0
   158→    SOVERSION 1
   159→)
   160→
   161→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162→# Executable: governance_halld (daemon)
   163→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   164→
   165→add_executable(governance_halld src/main.c)
   166→target_link_libraries(governance_halld PRIVATE governance_hall)
   167→
   168→install(TARGETS governance_hall governance_halld
   169→        EXPORT governance_hallTargets
   170→        RUNTIME DESTINATION bin
   171→        LIBRARY DESTINATION lib
   172→        ARCHIVE DESTINATION lib
   173→)
   174→
   175→install(DIRECTORY include/ DESTINATION include)
   176→
   177→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   178→# Unit/Integration Tests
   179→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   180→if(ENABLE_TESTING)
   181→    enable_testing()
   182→    # --- CMocka (pulled via FetchContent if missing) ---
   183→    FetchContent_Declare(
   184→        cmocka
   185→        GIT_REPOSITORY https://git.cryptomilk.org/projects/cmocka.git
   186→        GIT_TAG        cmocka-1.1.7
   187→    )
   188→    FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cmocka)
   189→
   190→    file(GLOB_RECURSE TEST_SRCS CONFIGURE_DEPENDS "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/tests/*.c")
   191→
   192→    add_executable(governance_hall_tests ${TEST_SRCS})
   193→    target_link_libraries(governance_hall_tests
   194→        PRIVATE governance_hall cmocka::cmocka
   195→    )
   196→    add_test(NAME governance_hall ALL COMMAND governance_hall_tests)
   197→endif()
   198→
   199→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   200→# Packaging
   201→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   202→include(CPack)
   203→set(CPACK_PACKAGE_NAME           "HoloCanvas-GovernanceHall")
   204→set(CPACK_PACKAGE_VENDOR         "HoloCanvas Collective")
   205→set(CPACK_PACKAGE_VERSION        "1.2.0")
   206→set(CPACK_PACKAGE_CONTACT        "dev@holocanvas.io")
   207→set(CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_SECTION "utils")
   208→set(CPACK_RPM_PACKAGE_GROUP      "Applications/Blockchain")
   209→set(CPACK_GENERATOR              "TGZ;DEB;RPM")
   210→cpack()
   211→
   212→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   213→# Developer Information
   214→# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   215→message(STATUS "Governance-Hall build configuration complete:")
   216→message(STATUS "  LTO                 : ${ENABLE_LTO}")
   217→message(STATUS "  clang-tidy          : ${ENABLE_CLANG_TIDY}")
   218→message(STATUS "  Coverage            : ${ENABLE_COVERAGE}")
   219→message(STATUS "  Tests               : ${ENABLE_TESTING}")
   220→

    61→typedef int64_t   i64;

   107→    HC_ERR_TIMEOUT       = -3,  /* Timeout / deadline exceeded        */
   108→    HC_ERR_NETWORK       = -4,  /* Network I/O error                  */
   109→    HC_ERR_CRYPTO        = -5,  /* Cryptographic failure              */
   110→    HC_ERR_INVALID_ARG   = -6,  /* Invalid argument / bad API usage   */
   111→    HC_ERR_STATE         = -7,  /* Invalid state for operation        */
   112→    HC_ERR_PERMISSION    = -8,  /* Permission / role denied           */

   124→        case HC_ERR_OOM:         return "Out of Memory";
   125→        case HC_ERR_TIMEOUT:     return "Timeout";
   126→        case HC_ERR_NETWORK:     return "Network Error";
   127→        case HC_ERR_CRYPTO:      return "Cryptography Error";
   128→        case HC_ERR_INVALID_ARG: return "Invalid Argument";
   129→        case HC_ERR_STATE:       return "Invalid State";
   130→        case HC_ERR_PERMISSION:  return "Permission Denied";
   131→        case HC_ERR_NOT_FOUND:   return "Not Found";
   132→        case HC_ERR_EXISTS:      return "Already Exists";

   134→    }
   135→}

   138→typedef enum hc_artifact_state_e {

   140→    HC_ARTIFACT_CURATED        = 1,
   141→    HC_ARTIFACT_AUCTION        = 2,
   142→    HC_ARTIFACT_FRACTIONALIZED = 3,

   144→    HC_ARTIFACT_ARCHIVED       = 5
   145→} hc_artifact_state_t;
   146→

   148→typedef enum hc_service_id_e {
   149→    HC_SVC_LEDGER_CORE    = 0x01,
   150→    HC_SVC_MINT_FACTORY   = 0x02,
   151→    HC_SVC_GALLERY_GATE   = 0x03,
   152→    HC_SVC_DEFI_GARDEN    = 0x04,

   162→} hc_tx_id_t;
   163→

   165→    hc_hash256_t hash;          /* Topic hash of event                */
   166→    u64          index;         /* Log index within block             */
   167→} hc_evt_id_t;
   168→
   169→/* --- JSON / CBOR Token Types (for streaming parsers) ---------------------- */
   170→typedef enum hc_token_type_e {
   171→    HC_TOK_NONE,
   172→    HC_TOK_BEGIN_OBJ,
   173→    HC_TOK_END_OBJ,
   174→    HC_TOK_BEGIN_ARR,
   175→    HC_TOK_END_ARR,
   176→    HC_TOK_KEY,
   177→    HC_TOK_STRING,
   178→    HC_TOK_NUMBER,
   179→    HC_TOK_TRUE,

   182→} hc_token_type_t;

   187→#define HC_MIN(a, b) ({          \
   188→    typeof(a) _a = (a);          \
   189→    typeof(b) _b = (b);          \
   190→    _a < _b ? _a : _b;           \

   192→
   193→#define HC_MAX(a, b) ({          \

/**
 * hc_event_dispatcher.h
 *
 * HoloCanvas – Shared Event Dispatcher Module
 * --------------------------------------------
 * A generic, reusable event dispatcher that centralizes the Kafka event
 * consumption loop and dispatching logic. Services can register event handlers
 * for specific event types and the dispatcher will route incoming messages
 * to the appropriate handlers.
 *
 * This module aims to eliminate duplicated boilerplate code across services
 * like governance_hall, mint_factory, and muse_observer.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   1. Create a dispatcher with event_dispatcher_create()
 *   2. Register handlers for event types with event_dispatcher_register_handler()
 *   3. Start the event loop with event_dispatcher_run()
 *   4. Clean up with event_dispatcher_destroy()
 *
 * Copyright (c) 2024 The HoloCanvas Contributors
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 */

#ifndef HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H
#define HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stddef.h>
#include <stdbool.h>

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

/* Include common error types from the shared library */
#include "../common/errors.h"

/* Forward declaration for protobuf-c message base type */
#ifndef PROTOBUF_C__MESSAGE__DEFINED
typedef struct ProtobufCMessage ProtobufCMessage;
#endif

/* ============================================================================
 * Type Definitions
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Function pointer type for event handlers.
 *
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "VOTE_CAST")
 * @param payload      Pointer to raw JSON payload string
 * @param payload_len  Length of the payload
 * @param user_context User-defined context pointer passed during registration
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate hc_error_t on failure
 */
typedef hc_error_t (*event_handler_func_t)(const char *event_type,
                                            const char *payload,
                                            size_t payload_len,
                                            void *user_context);

/**
 * Opaque struct representing the event dispatcher context.
 * The internal implementation is hidden from consumers.
 */
typedef struct EventDispatcher EventDispatcher;

/**
 * Configuration structure for creating an event dispatcher.
 */
typedef struct {
    const char *kafka_brokers;    /**< Comma-separated list of broker addresses */
    const char *topic;            /**< Kafka topic to subscribe to */
    const char *group_id;         /**< Consumer group identifier */
    void *user_context;           /**< User context passed to all handlers */
    int poll_timeout_ms;          /**< Kafka poll timeout in milliseconds (default: 500) */
    bool enable_auto_commit;      /**< Enable auto-commit of offsets (default: true) */
} EventDispatcherConfig;

/* ============================================================================
 * Public API
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Create a new event dispatcher instance.
 *
 * The dispatcher will connect to the specified Kafka brokers and subscribe
 * to the given topic. The user_context will be passed to all registered
 * event handlers.
 *
 * @param config Configuration structure with connection parameters
 *
 * @return Pointer to the newly created dispatcher, or NULL on failure
 */
EventDispatcher *event_dispatcher_create(const EventDispatcherConfig *config);

/**
 * Register an event handler for a specific event type.
 *
 * Multiple handlers can be registered for the same event type - they will
 * all be invoked when an event of that type is received.
 *
 * @param dispatcher   Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "PROPOSAL_CREATED")
 * @param handler      Function pointer to the event handler
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate error code on failure
 */
hc_error_t event_dispatcher_register_handler(EventDispatcher *dispatcher,
                                              const char *event_type,
                                              event_handler_func_t handler);

/**
 * Start the event dispatcher's main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function will block and continuously poll Kafka for messages,
 * deserializing them and dispatching to the appropriate registered handlers.
 * The loop will continue until event_dispatcher_stop() is called or a
 * fatal error occurs.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_run(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Signal the dispatcher to stop its main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function is thread-safe and can be called from a signal handler
 * or another thread. The event_dispatcher_run() function will return
 * after completing any in-progress message processing.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_stop(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Destroy the event dispatcher and release all resources.
 *
 * This function will stop the dispatcher if it's still running,
 * close the Kafka connection, and free all allocated memory including
 * the handler registry.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance (can be NULL)
 */
void event_dispatcher_destroy(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Get the user context associated with the dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return The user context pointer, or NULL if dispatcher is NULL
 */
void *event_dispatcher_get_context(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Check if the dispatcher is currently running.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return true if running, false otherwise
 */
bool event_dispatcher_is_running(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Install default signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that will stop
 * the given dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance to stop on signal
 */
void event_dispatcher_install_signal_handlers(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

#ifdef __cplusplus
} /* extern "C" */
#endif

#endif /* HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H */

 * Destroy the event dispatcher and release all resources.
 *
 * This function will stop the dispatcher if it's still running,
 * close the Kafka connection, and free all allocated memory including
 * the handler registry.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance (can be NULL)
 */
void event_dispatcher_destroy(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Get the user context associated with the dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return The user context pointer, or NULL if dispatcher is NULL
 */
void *event_dispatcher_get_context(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Check if the dispatcher is currently running.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return true if running, false otherwise
 */
bool event_dispatcher_is_running(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Install default signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that will stop
 * the given dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance to stop on signal
 */
void event_dispatcher_install_signal_handlers(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

#ifdef __cplusplus
} /* extern "C" */
#endif

#endif /* HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H */

 * hc_event_dispatcher.h
 *
 * HoloCanvas – Shared Event Dispatcher Module
 * --------------------------------------------
 * A generic, reusable event dispatcher that centralizes the Kafka event
 * consumption loop and dispatching logic. Services can register event handlers
 * for specific event types and the dispatcher will route incoming messages
 * to the appropriate handlers.
 *
 * This module aims to eliminate duplicated boilerplate code across services
 * like governance_hall, mint_factory, and muse_observer.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   1. Create a dispatcher with event_dispatcher_create()
 *   2. Register handlers for event types with event_dispatcher_register_handler()
 *   3. Start the event loop with event_dispatcher_run()
 *   4. Clean up with event_dispatcher_destroy()
 *
 * Copyright (c) 2024 The HoloCanvas Contributors
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 */

#ifndef HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H
#define HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stddef.h>
#include <stdbool.h>

#ifdef __cplusplus
extern "C" {
#endif

/* Include common error types from the shared library */
#include "../common/errors.h"

/* Forward declaration for protobuf-c message base type */
#ifndef PROTOBUF_C__MESSAGE__DEFINED
typedef struct ProtobufCMessage ProtobufCMessage;
#endif

/* ============================================================================
 * Type Definitions
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Function pointer type for event handlers.
 *
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "VOTE_CAST")
 * @param payload      Pointer to raw JSON payload string
 * @param payload_len  Length of the payload
 * @param user_context User-defined context pointer passed during registration
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate hc_error_t on failure
 */
typedef hc_error_t (*event_handler_func_t)(const char *event_type,
                                            const char *payload,
                                            size_t payload_len,
                                            void *user_context);

/**
 * Opaque struct representing the event dispatcher context.
 * The internal implementation is hidden from consumers.
 */
typedef struct EventDispatcher EventDispatcher;

/**
 * Configuration structure for creating an event dispatcher.
 */
typedef struct {
    const char *kafka_brokers;    /**< Comma-separated list of broker addresses */
    const char *topic;            /**< Kafka topic to subscribe to */
    const char *group_id;         /**< Consumer group identifier */
    void *user_context;           /**< User context passed to all handlers */
    int poll_timeout_ms;          /**< Kafka poll timeout in milliseconds (default: 500) */
    bool enable_auto_commit;      /**< Enable auto-commit of offsets (default: true) */
} EventDispatcherConfig;

/* ============================================================================
 * Public API
 * ============================================================================ */

/**
 * Create a new event dispatcher instance.
 *
 * The dispatcher will connect to the specified Kafka brokers and subscribe
 * to the given topic. The user_context will be passed to all registered
 * event handlers.
 *
 * @param config Configuration structure with connection parameters
 *
 * @return Pointer to the newly created dispatcher, or NULL on failure
 */
EventDispatcher *event_dispatcher_create(const EventDispatcherConfig *config);

/**
 * Register an event handler for a specific event type.
 *
 * Multiple handlers can be registered for the same event type - they will
 * all be invoked when an event of that type is received.
 *
 * @param dispatcher   Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 * @param event_type   String identifier for the event type (e.g., "PROPOSAL_CREATED")
 * @param handler      Function pointer to the event handler
 *
 * @return HC_OK on success, or an appropriate error code on failure
 */
hc_error_t event_dispatcher_register_handler(EventDispatcher *dispatcher,
                                              const char *event_type,
                                              event_handler_func_t handler);

/**
 * Start the event dispatcher's main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function will block and continuously poll Kafka for messages,
 * deserializing them and dispatching to the appropriate registered handlers.
 * The loop will continue until event_dispatcher_stop() is called or a
 * fatal error occurs.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_run(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Signal the dispatcher to stop its main consumption loop.
 *
 * This function is thread-safe and can be called from a signal handler
 * or another thread. The event_dispatcher_run() function will return
 * after completing any in-progress message processing.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 */
void event_dispatcher_stop(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Destroy the event dispatcher and release all resources.
 *
 * This function will stop the dispatcher if it's still running,
 * close the Kafka connection, and free all allocated memory including
 * the handler registry.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance (can be NULL)
 */
void event_dispatcher_destroy(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Get the user context associated with the dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return The user context pointer, or NULL if dispatcher is NULL
 */
void *event_dispatcher_get_context(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Check if the dispatcher is currently running.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance
 *
 * @return true if running, false otherwise
 */
bool event_dispatcher_is_running(const EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

/**
 * Install default signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that will stop
 * the given dispatcher.
 *
 * @param dispatcher Pointer to the dispatcher instance to stop on signal
 */
void event_dispatcher_install_signal_handlers(EventDispatcher *dispatcher);

#ifdef __cplusplus
} /* extern "C" */
#endif

#endif /* HC_EVENT_DISPATCHER_H */

arguments.content:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HoloCanvas :: shared :: CMake build configuration
#
# This CMakeLists.txt drives the compilation of the reusable "shared" component that is linked into all HoloCanvas
# micro-services.  It bundles common primitives such as:
#   • cryptographic helpers (OpenSSL wrappers)
#   • binary-safe serialization utilities (Protocol-Buffers / gRPC)
#   • event-stream abstractions (Kafka)
#   • event dispatcher (NEW: centralized event handling)
#   • state-machine helpers
#   • common error/ logging facilities
#
# The file is intentionally opinionated and enables modern-C best practices out of the box: C11, LTO, enable-all-warnings,
# sanitizers, unit-tests, documentation, packaging, and export-targets for downstream consumers.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20 FATAL_ERROR)

# 1. Project basics ---------------------------------------------------------------------
project(
    HoloCanvasShared
    VERSION 0.4.0
    DESCRIPTION "Common runtime & utils for the HoloCanvas micro-gallery blockchain (NFT)"
    LANGUAGES C
)

# 2. Build options ----------------------------------------------------------------------
option(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS "Build shared libraries instead of static ones" ON)
option(ENABLE_SANITIZER  "Enable UBSan / ASan checks (Debug only)"       OFF)
option(ENABLE_LTO        "Enable Link-Time-Optimization (Release only)" ON)
option(ENABLE_TESTS      "Build unit tests with CTest / Catch2"          ON)
option(ENABLE_COVERAGE   "Enable gcov/llvm-cov instrumentation"          OFF)

# 3. Toolchain sanity checks ------------------------------------------------------------
if(CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR STREQUAL CMAKE_BINARY_DIR)
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "In-source builds are disabled. Please create a separate build directory:\n"
        "  mkdir build && cd build && cmake .."
    )
endif()

# 4. Compiler setup ---------------------------------------------------------------------
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS OFF)

function(holo_enable_all_warnings TARGET)
    if (MSVC)
        target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE /W4 /WX)
    else()
        target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE
            -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror
            -Wshadow -Wpointer-arith -Wcast-align
            -Wstrict-prototypes -Wmissing-prototypes
            -Wwrite-strings -Wconversion
        )
    endif()
endfunction()

# 5. External dependencies --------------------------------------------------------------
find_package(Threads       REQUIRED)
find_package(OpenSSL       REQUIRED)
find_package(Protobuf      REQUIRED)
find_package(gRPC          REQUIRED)
find_package(ZLIB          REQUIRED)
# Jansson for JSON parsing (required by event_dispatcher)
find_package(PkgConfig REQUIRED)
pkg_check_modules(JANSSON REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET jansson)
# Kafka client (librdkafka) is shipped as a submodule
add_subdirectory(third_party/librdkafka EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)

# 6. Source layout ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Include the new event_dispatcher module
set(EVENT_DISPATCHER_SRCS
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/hc_event_dispatcher.c"
)

set(EVENT_DISPATCHER_HDRS
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/hc_event_dispatcher.h"
)

file(GLOB_RECURSE HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC  CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/crypto_wrapper/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/kafka_client/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/protocol/*.c"
)

file(GLOB_RECURSE HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/crypto_wrapper/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/kafka_client/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/protocol/*.h"
)

# Combine all sources
list(APPEND HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC ${EVENT_DISPATCHER_SRCS})
list(APPEND HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS ${EVENT_DISPATCHER_HDRS})

# 7. Auto-generate a version header -----------------------------------------------------
set(GIT_DESCRIBE "unknown")
find_package(Git QUIET)
if(GIT_FOUND)
    execute_process(
        COMMAND "${GIT_EXECUTABLE}" -C "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}" describe --tags --always --dirty
        OUTPUT_VARIABLE GIT_DESCRIBE
        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
    )
endif()

configure_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/version.h.in"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated/hc_version.h"
    @ONLY
)

# 8. Library target ---------------------------------------------------------------------
add_library(hc_shared ${HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC} ${HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS})
add_library(HoloCanvas::Shared ALIAS hc_shared)

target_include_directories(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}>
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated>
        $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>
)

target_link_libraries(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        OpenSSL::SSL
        OpenSSL::Crypto
        Threads::Threads
        ZLIB::ZLIB
        Protobuf::protobuf
        gRPC::grpc
        PkgConfig::JANSSON
    PRIVATE
        rdkafka
)

holo_enable_all_warnings(hc_shared)

target_compile_definitions(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        $<$<CONFIG:Debug>:HC_DEBUG=1>
        HC_VERSION=\"${PROJECT_VERSION}\"
        HC_GIT_DESCRIBE=\"${GIT_DESCRIBE}\"
)

# 8.1 Sanitizers ------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(ENABLE_SANITIZER AND CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang|GNU")
    target_compile_options(hc_shared PUBLIC -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
    target_link_options  (hc_shared PUBLIC -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
endif()

# 8.2 LTO ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
include(CheckIPOSupported)
if(ENABLE_LTO)
    check_ipo_supported(RESULT LTO_OK)
    if(LTO_OK)
        message(STATUS "Enabling LTO")

#   • cryptographic helpers (OpenSSL wrappers)
#   • binary-safe serialization utilities (Protocol-Buffers / gRPC)
#   • event-stream abstractions (Kafka)
#   • event dispatcher (NEW: centralized event handling)
#   • state-machine helpers
#   • common error/ logging facilities

# sanitizers, unit-tests, documentation, packaging, and export-targets for downstream consumers.

    HoloCanvasShared
    VERSION 0.4.0
    DESCRIPTION "Common runtime & utils for the HoloCanvas micro-gallery blockchain (NFT)"
    LANGUAGES C
)

option(ENABLE_LTO        "Enable Link-Time-Optimization (Release only)" ON)
option(ENABLE_TESTS      "Build unit tests with CTest / Catch2"          ON)
option(ENABLE_COVERAGE   "Enable gcov/llvm-cov instrumentation"          OFF)

        "In-source builds are disabled. Please create a separate build directory:\n"
        "  mkdir build && cd build && cmake .."

endif()

set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS OFF)

    if (MSVC)
        target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE /W4 /WX)
    else()
        target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE
            -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror
            -Wshadow -Wpointer-arith -Wcast-align
            -Wstrict-prototypes -Wmissing-prototypes
            -Wwrite-strings -Wconversion
        )
    endif()
endfunction()

find_package(OpenSSL       REQUIRED)
find_package(Protobuf      REQUIRED)
find_package(gRPC          REQUIRED)
find_package(ZLIB          REQUIRED)

    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/hc_event_dispatcher.c"

set(EVENT_DISPATCHER_HDRS
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/hc_event_dispatcher.h"
)

file(GLOB_RECURSE HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_SRC  CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/crypto_wrapper/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/kafka_client/*.c"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/protocol/*.c"
)

file(GLOB_RECURSE HOLOCANVAS_SHARED_HEADERS CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/common/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/crypto_wrapper/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/kafka_client/*.h"
     "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/protocol/*.h"

    @ONLY

    PUBLIC
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}>
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated>
        $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>
)

target_link_libraries(hc_shared
    PUBLIC
        OpenSSL::SSL
        OpenSSL::Crypto
        Threads::Threads
        ZLIB::ZLIB
        Protobuf::protobuf

        HC_GIT_DESCRIBE=\"${GIT_DESCRIBE}\"

    target_compile_options(hc_shared PUBLIC -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
    target_link_options  (hc_shared PUBLIC -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
endif()

# 8.2 LTO ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
include(CheckIPOSupported)
if(ENABLE_LTO)
    check_ipo_supported(RESULT LTO_OK)
    if(LTO_OK)
        message(STATUS "Enabling LTO")
        set_property(TARGET hc_shared PROPERTY INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION_RELEASE TRUE)
    endif()
endif()

if(ENABLE_COVERAGE AND CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang|GNU")
    target_compile_options(hc_shared PUBLIC -O0 -g --coverage)
    target_link_options  (hc_shared PUBLIC --coverage)
endif()

# 9. Installation -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    TARGETS hc_shared
    EXPORT  HoloCanvasSharedTargets
    ARCHIVE  DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    LIBRARY  DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME  DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}

configure_package_config_file(

install(

        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/HoloCanvasSharedConfigVersion.cmake"
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/HoloCanvas
)

# 10. Testing ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(ENABLE_TESTS)
    enable_testing()
    add_subdirectory(test)
endif()

# 11. Developer helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------
# clang-format target -------------------------------------------------------------------
find_program(CLANG_FORMAT_EXE NAMES clang-format)
if(CLANG_FORMAT_EXE)
    file(GLOB_RECURSE C_SOURCES_CLANGFORMAT
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/*.c"
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/*.h"
         "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/event_dispatcher/*.c"

if(CPPCHECK_EXE)

                -I ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}

    )

message(STATUS "HoloCanvas::Shared Configuration:")
